item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish this was real, stupid poop machines are everywhere and need to be taken out

item.title: introducing this to a friend rn
item.content:encoded:
He has a goose pfp

+1 boyos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: “Anti avian slurs” +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A goose racist. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to believe you're saying this because you're actually working on one…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's like Dan declared war on all Geese, and sent a freaking robot from the future to the past to rid the world of all of them to protect his sandwiches.

That being said, Dan Versus was a good show…

…..

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since they don't attack swans, they probably don't attack ducks either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necro post. How would this react to ducks? They are in the family of geese I think. (Could be wrong on that.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just imagine this thing on some open field, screaming wildly and running around as flocks of geese scatter before it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was intrigued by the mainlist title. I smashed the upvote button when I got to the line "anti-goose propoganda in French". I lost my shit at the actual quoted comments from the goose-centric murderbot. This is strange, lacks an explanation, and absolutely delightful.

Because seriously? Fuck geese.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This got a laugh out of me… Sometimes SCPs don't have to be scary or creepy to be good…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you win….everyone else can go home…this is the comment that killed me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me pretty sad to read, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine a music video of this thing just fucking up geese to this song, Achtung Panzer, by Raubtier. Full on low-scale war. Another cool thing, the translation is just as badass as the song sounds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ENGAGING GEESE AGGRESSORS. CANADA WILL NEVER FALL TO GEESE INVASION. GEESE ARE THE VERY DEFINITION OF FAILURE. FREEDOM IS THE SOVEREIGN RIGHT OF EVERY CANADIAN. INITIATING DIRECTIVE #1675: DESTROY ALL GEESE. DEATH IS A PREFERABLE ALTERNATIVE TO GEESE-DOM.

I…DIE… SO THAT… INNOCENT WATERFOWL… MAY.. LIVE…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the article, but I'm with UglyFlower on geese. I love geese. They are beautiful and majestic and have never to my knowledge caused any harm not caused by humans starting it (by being obnoxious in their presence, removing their habitat, etc…).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not all skips need a lesson or message. Sometimes a goose-killing robot is just a goose-killing robot.
Also, a lot of people hate geese because they coat everything in shit and are aggressive as fuck. Maybe geese are better behaved in Montreal, but here in the States, geese are nothing but monstrous avians deserving only of death. Also, they drove off the local duck population in my area, and nobody fucks with my ducks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh great. Another animal lolrandomness. This article is just asking for a -J suffix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about it and normally I'm pretty stubborn, but I actually agree with you here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a wagon that people can jump on for anything that's been done. I'm from Montreal Canada, the home of the goose, and I love geese. I don't at all understand the "universal hatred of geese" that your talking about. The dog killing one brought a message, which was that "people will do anything to selfishly obtain even the smallest reward." To me, this just comes off as a thing that kills geese for no reason. I didn't feel anything from it, and unless I'm missing something, there wasn't any kind of life-lesson/message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take your word for it. The last goose I dealt with was too scared of me to try and bite. He just ran off, clearing the way for my sister's car.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because I live in Michigan. Anything that kills the damned grey footballs is great in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it's not hard to imagine such a thing being created in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think that's entirely fair. 1459's "charm" came from the hilarious and macabre ways it went about interpreting different kill methods provided. It makes you upvote it in spite of the fact that everyone loves puppies.

This one's charm lies in the fact that so many of us can understand the hatred for geese, and a goose killbot sounds like something I wouldn't mind existing in real life. I really doubt Aelanna was just trying to hop on the "thing that kills animals" wagon, if there is such a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see you are following the working trend of "apparatuses that murder animals". Well written, but the originality of it bugs me, considering its coming so soon after the "murder a dog in different ways" scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The propaganda really makes this, but I think I like this because I can flat-out understand why it was made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect. It's a completely anomalous thing that needs to be contained and has internal consistency on all levels. And, perhaps most refreshingly, it's not connected to anything at all. No GoI reference, no canon reference, no ongoing storyline, it's not even a foot long. I like lots of (and have mostly written) SCPs that are different from all of those things, but it's really refreshing to get one that's just…itself. Like Beauregard, or the Tickle Monster. But it still contains everything it needs. Upvoted now and unto infinity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Swans, man, it should kill swans. Swans are evil. I used to live in Oxford where people go punting along the river in summer, and if you so much as smell of food they will mob you like a bad horror movie and not go away until someone else shows up to become their next victim. Swans need to die, or at least switch to a colour like crimson that suits them better. I can completely believe swans built this thing to kill off the competition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The blight of Canada Geese in Virginia is simply deplorable. While set up for a yard sale, the field across the street had frigging 200 birds just wandering about, eating whatever the hell it is they were picking out of the grass, and left a mess that can only be described as a "Lovecraftian horror".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New GOI: Swans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Awww yiss" seems like a really appropriate response in the context of this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done. I can't fight against that kind of sentiment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please please please just change it back.

Raikonos' post made me want to upvote this a second time.

EDIT: thought Raikonos was Randomini.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this post and now feel slightly bad for changing it from swans to turkeys due to another misunderstanding.

._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last line was what made this for me and secured my upvote, because suddenly the robot's existence makes a bizarre kind of sense. It kills geese on sight, we know that much. But its description says that under all other circumstances it remains completely passive, remaining entirely unresponsive to humans (and, I would assume, other animals) unless they directly endanger it. And yet, when presented with a swan, it spoke. And what it said was "nothing to see here, move along". Why would it say that to a swan, when it had shown no reaction to humans?

Because it was built by swans.

It's an advanced police robot built by the swans to combat their hated enemy, the geese. And being made by swans, its programming interprets swans as civilians. Humans, on the other hand, don't provoke any sort of response. They're not geese, so they don't need to be killed. They're not swans, so they don't need to be protected or spoken to. Humans are just animals, not requiring any action unless they begin to endanger the robot. Same way it'd react to a dog.

But yeah, I just love the idea of a swan-built anti-goose military killbot, and the way it all clicked together at the end was very satisfying. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For too long have we suffered under threat of anser-bolshevik power, with their helpers in the Chen and Branta plutocracies! One Fowl! One Flock! One Leader!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Errr… it's an artificially intelligent machine capable of autonomously waging an unrelenting war against… geese.
How is this not anomalous ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can one-up you on that:

http://www.ksl.com/?sid=25043694

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Canada geese, not Canadian geese.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an okay article, taken for all in all. I just don't think it's anomalous enough to be a skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is right on the edge of my personal mainlist silliness threshold. It gets pushed into upvote territory because of my burning hatred for canada geese. I feel bad letting my hate guide decisions, and you should feel bad for exploiting my goose-animus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Canadian geese generally do not have even a single fuck to give. My area is apparently on a major migration path and so twice a year we have huge flocks of geese come through. Geese will freely and without shame wander slowly across busy streets. I once saw traffic brought to a stand-still for 5 minutes because a goose got it in its head to stop in the middle of the road in order to preen itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP makes perfect sense to anyone who has ever had Canada geese on their property. They eat all your grass/plants, shit everywhere, and are generally evil.

The only good goose is a roasted goose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tools provided: 1 acetylene torch ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, needs more one-liners and quips ("your goose is cooked")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anything well written that deals with the demise of geese gets my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my mom's farm, we used geese as an alarm, since they would make so much noise if someone got close.

Other than that, they are really agressive and must be purged, unless they are mute by the looks of it.

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
understand. A goose tried to attack me once, but was received with an iron-enhanced kick1 in its whity, birdy ass… Ok, maybe I didn't kick him that hard, btu the fucker tried to btie my nuts! (just like with crayne's grandpa).

Footnotes
1. AKA security boots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Geese are a blight that must be purged."

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I went to downvote this at first until I reminded myself that not every SCP demands an explanation or some kind of rhyme or reason.

Sometimes it's fun for something to make absolutely no sense, it makes me wonder where the author ever came up with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. The good kind of random is like a quirky, unpredictable BFF who keeps surprising you with goofy fun and adventures. I've upvoted many random-ass SCPs for feeling like this.

The bad kind of random is like a dementia-addled grandparent who spouts completely unrelated words and then guffaws mechanically at what was apparently a joke, while twitching worryingly in their wheelchair. That is what this SCP feels like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like random. Sometimes you just need to appreciate random.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess any normal person who already meet a goose has dreamed at least once of a geese-killing machine…

By the way, if you want actual french translation of the propaganda, just ask ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My grandfather was actually bitten in the nuts by one of these things. Not kidding, I was there. The most fucked-up thing to witness as a toddler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, you amused me. Which, granted, isn't saying much, but it does get you a +1. I do agree that you could milk this for far more though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is silly, it's random, and overall I agree with all the above points. But you know what? I fucking hate geese. They are evil, disgusting birds with no redeeming qualities to them whatsoever. On that principle alone I'm giving this a +1, and if you think I'm overreacting, then you've never been attacked by one of these hissing motherfuckers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded. It's a fantastic base idea, it just doesn't quite push the envelope far enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs a lot more punch to be effective, which is sad, because it's the kind of dumb shit I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, me neither. -1.

It's just random.
